facts.text: Flatbush gets its name from the Dutch Vlacke Bos, meaning “flat woods,” and was founded in 1651 as part of New Netherland.
Historic districts like Prospect Park South and Ditmas Park protect early suburban charm, while the Kings Theatre, reopened in 2015, once hosted vaudeville shows.
The century‑old Avenue H station on the BMT Brighton Line, a New York City landmark, still serves Flatbush commuters.
The Flatbush Reformed Dutch Church, standing at Church and Flatbush Avenues, has been a community anchor since the 18th century.
Since 1975, the Flatbush Development Corporation has championed preservation, small‑business support, and housing renewal in the area.
